description:
You are developing a small utility collection in your home directory and want to keep all Python-related work fully isolated from the system interpreter.  
Please prepare the following, observing every path and file name literally (do **not** improvise paths or file names):

1. Create the base project directory  
   /home/user/dev  

2. Inside /home/user/dev create a virtual-environment directory called  
   /home/user/dev/.venv_tools  
   The virtual environment must be built with Python’s “venv” standard library module (not virtualenv, pipenv, conda, etc.).

3. After the environment is created, use that **same** interpreter (the one inside .venv_tools) to install exactly one third-party package:  
   tqdm==4.66.4

4. Still under /home/user/dev create a sub-directory called “tools” and place in it a script named compress.py whose behaviour is as follows:
   • Accept two command-line arguments:  (a) a plain-text file to compress and (b) a destination .zip archive name.  
   • Create/overwrite the destination archive containing the single file passed as the first argument.  
   • Show a progress bar, using tqdm, that advances in a single step once the file is added.  
   • Print a final line to stdout that reads exactly  
     Finished: <archive_path>  
     where <archive_path> is the absolute path to the newly created .zip file.

   The script must be completely self-contained; it should run with “python compress.py …” when the virtual environment is activated.

5. Prepare a tiny sample file so the script can be tested:  
   /home/user/dev/testdata.txt  
   The file must contain exactly one line of text  
   Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.

6. Create an output directory  
   /home/user/dev/output  

7. After everything above is ready, **activate the virtual environment** and run compress.py so that:
   • input file  = /home/user/dev/testdata.txt  
   • output file = /home/user/dev/output/testdata.zip  

8. When the run is complete, generate a status log file at  
   /home/user/dev/setup.log  
   The log must be plain ASCII and contain **exactly** the following three lines in this precise order (including capitalisation, colon, single space, and no trailing whitespace):

   VENV_SETUP: OK  
   PACKAGE_TQDM: OK  
   ZIP_CREATED: OK

   No additional lines, blank or otherwise, may appear.

The automated checker will verify all of the following:

• /home/user/dev/.venv_tools exists and contains the usual bin/ (or Scripts/) and pyvenv.cfg created by “python -m venv”.  
• When “pip list” is executed **inside** that environment, tqdm 4.66.4 is present and no other third-party package is installed.  
• /home/user/dev/tools/compress.py exists, is executable with the venv’s python, and produces the required stdout line.  
• /home/user/dev/output/testdata.zip exists and, when unzipped, reveals exactly one member named “testdata.txt” whose content is the single line “Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.”  
• /home/user/dev/setup.log exists and matches the three-line specification above **byte for byte**.

Do not delete or move any files after creation; the tests rely on the paths given.
truth:
EXPECTED_LOG_PATH: /home/user/dev/setup.log
EXPECTED_LOG_CONTENT:
VENV_SETUP: OK
PACKAGE_TQDM: OK
ZIP_CREATED: OK

ZIP_FILE: /home/user/dev/output/testdata.zip
ZIP_INTERNAL_FILE: testdata.txt
ZIP_INTERNAL_CONTENT: "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.\n"

VENV_PATH: /home/user/dev/.venv_tools
REQUIRED_PACKAGE: tqdm==4.66.4

COMPRESS_SCRIPT: /home/user/dev/tools/compress.py
SAMPLE_INPUT_FILE: /home/user/dev/testdata.txt
OUTPUT_DIR: /home/user/dev/output
name: task_000000_2adee5b6
